etymology.description: The word 美麗 (měilì, 'beautiful') is a compound word in Traditional Chinese, referring to someone or something that has an aesthetic appealing quality.
etymology.interpretation: In 美麗 (měilì, 'beautiful'), 美(美) (měi, 'beautiful; pretty; pleasing') suggests the inherent quality of physical or aesthetic appeal, conveying beauty in form or appearance. 麗(麗) (lì, 'beautiful; elegant; magnificent') emphasizes elegance and grace. Together, they describe a state or quality of enhanced beauty, where elegance and appealing form combine to create an impression of beauty.

examples.chinese: 这座城市非常美丽(這座城市非常美麗)。
她有一头美丽的长发(她有一頭美麗的長髮)。
examples.pinyin: Tā yǒu yītóu měilì de chángfà.

examples.chinese: 美丽的花园吸引了很多游客(美麗的花園吸引了很多遊客)。
examples.pinyin: Měilì de huāyuán xīyǐn le hěn duō yóukè.
examples.english: The beautiful garden attracted many tourists.
